title: A meal I cooked for someone else
summary: Tell the story of one meal you cooked for another person.
topic: A meal I cooked for someone else
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences about a meal you cooked for someone else. Say what you made, for whom, and how it went. Use past simple and past continuous.
hintStarters: Two years ago, I decided to cook a proper meal for…
I had never made this dish before, so I…
While the onions were frying, I realised that…
sampleText: Two years ago, I decided to cook a proper birthday dinner for my flatmate, Daniela, in our small apartment in Porto. She was working long hours that week, so I wanted to do something that showed I was thinking of her. I chose a Spanish tomato and chickpea stew because she once told me she loved it as a child. I went to the market on Saturday morning and bought fresh tomatoes, two onions, garlic, and a tin of chickpeas. Back in the kitchen, I chopped the onions and fried them slowly in olive oil. While the onions were frying, I found her old handwritten recipe card inside a cookbook on the shelf. That felt like a lucky sign. I added the tomatoes and let everything simmer for forty minutes. The kitchen filled with a warm, deep smell. When Daniela came home, she stopped at the door and said, 'That smells exactly like my mother's kitchen.' We ate at the small table by the window with a candle between us. She told me it was the best birthday gift she received that year. I did not say anything, but I felt very proud of that simple pot of stew.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một bữa tối sinh nhật thực sự đàng hoàng

sampleAnnotations.phrase: showed I was thinking of her
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: cho thấy tôi đang nghĩ đến cô ấy

sampleAnnotations.phrase: she once told me she loved it
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: cô ấy đã từng nói với tôi rằng cô ấy thích món đó

sampleAnnotations.phrase: fried them slowly in olive oil
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: xào chúng chậm trong dầu ô liu
Trong khi hành tây đang được xào
tấm thẻ công thức nấu ăn viết tay
để mọi thứ đun nhỏ lửa
cảm thấy rất tự hào về

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past continuous for background action
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng past continuous ('were frying', 'was working') để mô tả hành động đang diễn ra trong nền, trong khi past simple ('found', 'added') dùng cho các sự kiện cụ thể xảy ra vào lúc đó.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use past continuous ('were frying', 'was working') for an ongoing background action, while past simple ('found', 'added') marks the specific event that happened at that moment.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Reported speech with 'told me'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'She once told me she loved it' dùng past simple trong mệnh đề phụ vì thời gian đã lùi về quá khứ (backshift).
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'She once told me she loved it' uses past simple in the reported clause because the tense shifts back (backshift) when reporting someone's past words.

sampleTranslationVi: Hai năm trước, tôi quyết định nấu một bữa tối sinh nhật thực sự đàng hoàng cho người bạn cùng phòng, Daniela, trong căn hộ nhỏ của chúng tôi ở Porto. Cô ấy đang làm việc nhiều giờ trong tuần đó, nên tôi muốn làm điều gì đó cho thấy tôi đang nghĩ đến cô ấy. Tôi chọn món hầm cà chua và đậu gà kiểu Tây Ban Nha vì cô ấy đã từng nói với tôi rằng cô ấy thích món đó hồi còn nhỏ. Tôi đi chợ vào sáng thứ Bảy và mua cà chua tươi, hai củ hành tây, tỏi, và một hộp đậu gà. Về đến bếp, tôi thái hành tây và xào chậm trong dầu ô liu. Trong khi hành tây đang được xào, tôi tìm thấy tấm thẻ công thức nấu ăn viết tay cũ của cô ấy bên trong một cuốn sách nấu ăn trên kệ. Điều đó như một dấu hiệu may mắn. Tôi thêm cà chua vào và để mọi thứ đun nhỏ lửa trong bốn mươi phút. Căn bếp ngập tràn một mùi thơm ấm áp, đậm đà. Khi Daniela về nhà, cô ấy dừng lại ở cửa và nói, 'Mùi này giống hệt bếp của mẹ mình.' Chúng tôi ăn ở chiếc bàn nhỏ cạnh cửa sổ với một ngọn nến ở giữa. Cô ấy nói với tôi đó là món quà sinh nhật tốt nhất cô ấy nhận được trong năm đó. Tôi không nói gì, nhưng tôi cảm thấy rất tự hào về nồi hầm đơn giản đó.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural opening for a past cooking story.
options: Two years ago, I decide to cook a birthday dinner for my flatmate.
Two years ago, I decided to cook a proper birthday dinner for my flatmate.
Two years ago, I am deciding to cook a birthday dinner for my flatmate.
Two years ago, I have decided to cook a birthday dinner for my flatmate.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Two years ago' requires past simple. 'Decided' is correct; 'decide', 'am deciding', and 'have decided' don't fit past finished time.
mcQuestions.hint: Which tense goes with 'two years ago'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses past continuous for a background action?
options: While the onions fried, I found the recipe card.
While the onions were frying, I found the recipe card.
While the onions were frying, I was finding the recipe card.
While the onions are frying, I found the recipe card.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past continuous sets the ongoing background ('were frying'); past simple marks the specific event ('found').
mcQuestions.hint: Background = past continuous. Specific event = past simple.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct verb for cooking food slowly over low heat.
options: boil
fry
simmer
bake
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Simmer' means to cook liquid gently just below the boiling point. The others describe different cooking methods.
mcQuestions.hint: Which word means 'cook gently at low heat in liquid'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the sentence that correctly reports what Daniela said.
options: She told me it was the best birthday gift she received that year.
She told me it is the best birthday gift she receives that year.
She said me it was the best birthday gift she received that year.
She told to me it was the best birthday gift she received that year.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Tell someone' (not 'tell to someone' or 'say someone') + reported clause in past simple is the correct pattern.
mcQuestions.hint: What follows 'told'? What tense goes in the reported clause?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrase describes cutting an onion into small pieces?
options: stirred the onion
simmered the onion
chopped the onion
seasoned the onion
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Chopped' means cut into small pieces with a knife.
mcQuestions.hint: Which verb means 'to cut into small pieces with a knife'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the sentence where 'because' is used correctly.
options: I chose the stew because of she loved it as a child.
I chose the stew because she once told me she loved it as a child.
I chose the stew because she once told me she loves it as a child.
I chose the stew because she once tells me she loved it as a child.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Because' is followed by a full clause (subject + verb). 'She once told me' is past simple, correct with the main past-simple verb.
mcQuestions.hint: 'Because' needs subject + verb after it, not 'because of'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence shows contrast between effort and a simple result?
options: I felt very proud of that simple pot of stew.
I felt very proud of the complicated sauce.
The stew was complicated and took many steps.
Simple food is always worse than elaborate food.
mcQuestions.explanation: The writer is proud of something 'simple', which shows that effort matters more than complexity.
mcQuestions.hint: Which option shows pride in something ordinary?

mcQuestions.prompt: Select the phrase that describes food-smell filling a room.
options: The kitchen emptied with a warm smell.
The kitchen filled with a warm, deep smell.
The kitchen was filling to a warm smell.
The kitchen full with a warm smell.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Filled with' is the correct collocation: the room becomes full of something.
mcQuestions.hint: Which phrase uses the correct verb and preposition?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best expresses why the writer cooked the meal?
options: I wanted to do something that showed I was thinking of her.
I wanted to do something that shows I think of her.
I wanted to do something that show I was thinking of her.
I want to do something that showed I was thinking of her.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'wanted' + past continuous 'was thinking' fits a finished past event.
mcQuestions.hint: Match all verb tenses to the past context.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the sentence that uses 'grateful' correctly.
options: She was grateful that I cooked for her.
She was grateful for I cooked for her.
She was grateful to I cooked for her.
She was grateful because of I cooked for her.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Grateful that + clause' is the natural structure. 'Grateful for' is followed by a noun, not a full clause.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the word that means food left over after a meal.
options: recipe
effort
leftovers
portions
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Leftovers' are the food that remains after everyone has eaten.
mcQuestions.hint: Which word means 'food that remains after the meal'?

criticalThinkingQuestion: Have you ever cooked for someone to show care rather than just to feed them? How is cooking for others different from cooking for yourself?
